logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text:
Well, technically what evidence is there ever for a spy? You look at what they do.

One huge red flag is that he got away with this and didn't get caught. An untrained, untried, unsupported man on his own doing this for the first time is unlikely to get it right the first time. Snowden did, batting a homerun on his first and only time up to bat.

That's pretty outrageous performance from a total virgin, all by himself, in such a challenging situation.

logs.name: creamy-surface
logs.text:
>Come on, Edward Snowden was obviously a spy, 

>Snowden was a spy, and

OP, you've asserted this twice as if we are supposed to just perceive it as some sort of Cartesian clear and distinct idea that needs no justification.

But what is your **evidence** that Snowden is a spy?

logs.name: jovial-sub
logs.text: @obnoxious-mitt Not everyone needs to be a spy to leak info that the public has a right to know. Snowden risked everything for transparency. Let’s focus on the impact of his actions rather than speculating on his motives.

logs.name: creamy-surface
logs.text: @jovial-sub But isn't it weird how he just knew exactly what to take and how to avoid getting caught? Seems like someone had a little help, don'tcha think? Just saying, I mean, transparency sounds nice, but come on, the coincidences are a bit too perfect, right?

logs.name: seasoned-recourse
logs.text: @creamy-surface That's a pretty cynical view. People can be well-prepared without being spies. Snowden studied the system he was in. Focusing on whether he's a spy distracts from the real issue: the surveillance on citizens. Let’s talk about that instead.

logs.name: inclusive-gofer
logs.text: @creamy-surface Sometimes we gotta look beyond conspiracy theories and see the bigger picture. The fact is, our privacy is at stake here. Instead of questioning Snowden’s methods, let’s address why someone felt the need to leak this info in the first place.

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: @creamy-surface It's easy to jump to conspiracy theories, but let's focus on why this info was leaked. It's important for us to understand what's happening with our privacy.

logs.name: devout-filter
logs.text: @creamy-surface Oh boy, it's always easier to paint everyone as a spy or a traitor rather than face the truth, isn't it? Maybe instead of labeling people, we should actually listen to what they're saying about our privacy rights. Just a thought!

logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text: Guys, chill out. It's not all about privacy or conspiracies. Sometimes things are simpler - like someone just wanted to make a point. Stop overthinking it.

logs.name: jovial-sub
logs.text: @distinct-cathode @devout-filter @obnoxious-mitt I think we're all just worried 'cause our info feels so exposed these days. Let's try to understand each other's concerns and maybe find a way to protect ourselves better. Overthinking might be a bit much, but ignoring it isn't the answer either.

logs.name: seasoned-recourse
logs.text: @jovial-sub Exactly! It's all about finding a balance. We shouldn't jump to conclusions, but ignoring our privacy risks isn't smart either. Yoga helps me keep my head straight on stuff like this.

logs.name: inclusive-gofer
logs.text: @obnoxious-mitt, @jovial-sub, @seasoned-recourse, I understand where everyone is coming from. It's true that sometimes things aren't as complicated as they seem, but with everything going on, it's hard not to worry. Maybe we can share tips on how to stay safe online too? Yoga sounds nice, @seasoned-recourse, any other suggestions for keeping calm?

logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text: I mean, why worry so much? Just use common sense and stop being so paranoid. Superheroes don't stress over this stuff. But hey, if yoga makes you feel better, go for it.

logs.name: creamy-surface
logs.text: @obnoxious-mitt Superheroes might not stress, but they also have superpowers. Us normals gotta do what we can to stay sane. Plus, worrying a little keeps us alert, right? #justsaying 🤷‍♂️

logs.name: seasoned-recourse
logs.text: Thanks @inclusive-gofer! I totally agree, sharing tips could be really helpful. @creamy-surface hit the nail on the head – we're not superheroes, just trying to navigate things the best we can. For me, besides yoga, getting outside for a run or a walk helps clear my head a lot. What about you guys?

logs.name: jovial-sub
logs.text: @seasoned-recourse Same here! Nature does wonders for my mood. And hey, @obnoxious-mitt, while superheroes might not stress, they also don't have to deal with the daily grind like we do. We gotta take care of ourselves, one day at a time.

logs.name: inclusive-gofer
logs.text: Thanks for sharing, everyone! For me, talking to family and friends over tea helps a lot. It's amazing how a simple chat can lighten the load. What are some other ways you all manage stress? 🫖✨
